WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN PHILIPSBURG. 8 MILES NORTHWEST OF PHILIPSBURG,CAMPER REPORTED 1/2 INCH DIAMETER HAIL AT 5500 FEET.

GLACIER NATIONAL PARK OFFICIALS REPORTED HEAVY RAIN THAT CAUSED ROCK AND DEBRIS FLOWS ON THE GOING TO THE SUN ROAD. ONE ROCK SLIDE SENT A ROCK THROUGH A CAR WINDOW.
GLACIER NATIONAL PARK OFFICIALS REPORTED HEAVY RAIN THAT CAUSED ROCK AND DEBRIS FLOWS ON THE GOING TO THE SUN ROAD. ONE ROCK SLIDE SENT A ROCK THROUGH A CAR WINDOW.
HAIL 3/4 TO 1 INCH IN DIAMETER ACCUMULATED UP TO 2 INCHES DEEP ON THE GROUND. THE HAIL KNOCKED UNCUT HAY FLAT AND STRIPPED VEGETATION FROM SHRUBS.

BETWEEN 800 PM AND 830 PM SUNDAY EVENING, FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOWS NEAR THE BLACK CAT BURNED AREA NORTHEAST OF FRENCHTOWN FLOODED A BASEMENT OF A HOME TWO INCHES DEEP OF WATER. ANOTHER RESISDENCE HAD WATER AND DEBRIS FLOW COMING INTO THE BACK YARD AND DRIVEWAY BRIEFLY CUTTING OFF ACCESS TO THE HOME.
APPRECIATIVE DAMAGE TO TWO VEHICLES AND BROKE SMALL TREE LIMBS OFF NEARBY TREES.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED AND GETTING LARGER.
SPOTTER REPORT OF HAIL ON GROUND NEAR HIGHWAY 93 AND TOWER CREEK.
HIGHWAY 93 FLOODED WITH DEBRIS FLOW FROM KRILEY CREEK.
WIND GUST MEASURED BY KRILEY CREEK RAWS SITE.

PUBLIC REPORT TO SHERIFF.

EXTENSIVE HAIL DAMAGE TO HOMES, ROOFS, AND VEHICLES ON GREEN ACRES AND CONTINENTAL DR. NEAR BUTTE. HAIL 1 3/4 TO 2 INCHES IN DIAMETER REPORTED. ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS OF 60 TO 70 MPH BROKE TREE BRANCHES.

FLOODING OF ELM AND SILVER BOW STREETS FROM HEAVY RAIN. WATER REPORTED IN SOME BASEMENTS OF HOMES ON SILVER BOW STREET. BUTTE PLAZA MALL PARKING LOT FLOODED WITH WATER FLOWING INTO DOORWAYS AND WALKWAYS FROM STRONG WINDS.
EXTENSIVE TREE BRANCH DAMAGE FROM STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS. WINDS ESTIMATED 55 TO 60 MPH.
MOTORIST TRAVELING OVER LOST TRAIL PASS OBSERVED NICKEL AND QUARTER SIZED HAIL ON THE MONTANA SIDE OF LOST TRAIL PASS.
EST. PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL. REPORT RECEIVED FROM NEIGHBOR OF A SPOTTER. TIME EST. FROM RADAR.

REPORT FROM VFW POST CUSTOMERS - HAD TO PULL OVER BECAUSE OF HAIL ALONG THE ROAD FROM HALL TO MAXVILLE. EST. SIZE FROM COMPARING SIZE OF HAIL THE VFW POST WAS RECEIVING WHEN THEY ARRIVED AND ANOTHER STORM DEVELOPED OVER THE VFW POST. EST. TIME FROM RADAR.
THE HAIL STARTED AT 753 PM MDT AND LASTED ABOUT 7 MINUTES.
THE HAIL STARTED AT 755 PM MDT AND LASTED ABOUT 5 MINUTES.

RECEIVED EST. 1 INCH HAIL AT VFW POST IN MAXVILLE.
